title: The Great Catalonian Switcheroo
theme: Triple inversion at Camiral — the serial Spoon collector wins the Trophy, the reigning Spoon holder wins the Jacket, and the defending Trophy champion collects the Spoon. The Stableford forgives carnage; the gross does not.
opening_hook: Open on Alex Baker writing an 11 on the card at R2 H18 — the par-5 that should have ended a Trophy challenge — then pan out to reveal he still won by 8, and that all three pots had changed hands with the people who held them last year. The body is organised around the inversion, not the calendar.
foreshadow: Alex Baker arriving with 3 prior Wooden Spoons and Spoons in 2 of the last 3 TEGs
Gregg Williams arriving as the reigning Wooden Spoon holder from TEG 17
Jon Baker arriving as the defending Trophy champion from TEG 17
Alex's R1 catalogue of disasters (7 at the 2nd, 9 at the 18th) already piling up while he led

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Wire-to-wire. Alex took outright Trophy lead at R1 H5 and was never headed for the next 67 holes, finishing on 169 pts to win by 8 over John Patterson. Margin held through four rounds despite an extraordinary catalogue of personal carnage — a 7, three 8s, two 9s, a 10 and an 11, plus the course-worst 106 at the Tour. In Stableford none of it killed him; the blanks simply zeroed those holes and the points kept stacking elsewhere (b03's 21-point heater R3 H11–16 the loudest statement).

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Jon Baker led after R1 and R2 (by 2 both times); Williams sat 3rd then 2nd, hunting. At R3 H2 Williams took outright Jacket lead (b12) on the back of a four-par opening burst (b29) and never gave it back, building +8 by R3's close and +14 by the finish — the 6th-largest Jacket margin all-time. Cushioned further by matching Mullin's 84 at the Stadium in R4 (his new personal best at the course).

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: The defending Trophy champion. Through R1 and R2 the bottom slot changed hands six times (Mullin, Patterson, Williams, Baker, Mullin again). At R3 H2 Jon Baker triple-bogeyed the 2nd to drop to last (b27) and stayed there — bottom in R3, bottom in R4, finishing on 126 pts. His R4 H7–11 stretch (b48, 10 shots bled in five holes) sealed it.

headline_candidates: Catalonia Opens with a Crash and a Cushion
Alex Baker Builds a Lead Out of Wreckage
Mullin's Eleven, and a Baker in Front Anyway
rounds.chosen_headline: Alex Baker Builds a Lead Out of Wreckage
rounds.angle: Tour course (5th TEG round at the venue). Alex Baker grabs outright Trophy lead at H5 and ends the day 12 clear despite a 7 at the 2nd and a 9 at the 18th — Stableford's mercy already on display. Mullin's 11 at the 15th drops him to Spoon bottom; Patterson bleeds 22 shots H3–10; Williams quad-bogeys the 7th and 17th; Jon Baker leads the Jacket.

headline_candidates: Eleven on the Last, Still Leading
Alex Baker Sets a Course Record. The Wrong One.
106, an 8 at a Par-3, and an 11 — and the Lead Holds
rounds.chosen_headline: Alex Baker Sets a Course Record. The Wrong One.
rounds.angle: Tour course again (6th TEG round). Alex's 106 establishes a new Tour course-worst (cr02), built on an 8 at the par-3 16th and an 11 at the 18th — and his Trophy lead still only narrows from 12 to 11. Jon Baker briefly takes outright Jacket lead at H9 (b46); Williams's H13–16 par run (b26) reels it back. Spoon hot-potato: Williams to Jon Baker to Jon Baker to Mullin in 16 holes.

headline_candidates: Mullin Breaks the Course; Williams Breaks the Field
Two Hours That Decided Two Pots
rounds.angle: Move to the Stadium (6th TEG round there). The two decisive moments arrive in the same R3 H2: Williams pars to take outright Jacket lead (b12), Jon Baker triple-bogeys to drop to Spoon bottom (b27). Mullin then writes the round of the tournament — 84 gross, a new Stadium course record across 27 prior visits (cr01). Alex Baker's 21-point burst H11–16 (b03) restores the Trophy buffer to 12 after a brutal H6–10 (b43).

headline_candidates: Williams Coronated, Jon Baker Spooned, Alex Baker Crashes In
rounds.angle: Stadium again (7th TEG round). Williams matches Mullin's 84 to seal the Jacket by 14 — his Stadium PB by 8 shots. Alex Baker finds another 10 (H14) and an 8 (H17), still wins the Trophy by 8. Jon Baker's H7–11 stretch (b48) closes off any Spoon escape. All three reigning-holders' roles inverted on the same hillside.

players.arc: The serial Spooner crowned. Three prior Wooden Spoons and Spoons in two of the last three TEGs, and yet from R1 H5 he led the Trophy and was never headed — winning by 8 in spite of (not because of) a catalogue that included a 7, three 8s, two 9s, a 10, an 11, and the new Tour course-worst of 106.

From reigning Wooden Spoon holder to Green Jacket champion in twelve months. Patient through two rounds in the Jacket chase, then took outright lead at R3 H2 and ran the margin out to 14 — a top-six all-time Jacket margin — capped by an 84 at the Stadium, his course PB by 8.

The defending Trophy champion collects the Spoon. Led the Jacket after R1 and R2, then went backwards: a triple at R3 H2 sent him to last and he never climbed off it, sealed by bleeding 10 shots H7–11 in R4.

Wrote the round of the tournament — 84 gross at the Stadium in R3, a new course record across 27 prior visits, and his Stadium PB by 7 — and finished Jacket runner-up by 14. Undone elsewhere by R1's 11 at the 15th and a R2 that was 10 shots worse than his last visit to the Tour.

Quiet Trophy runner-up at 8 back, with personal bests at both PGA Catalunya courses. Bled 22 shots H3–10 in R1 and another 11 H6–10 in R2 — but unlike the front-runners, kept the worst contained to a couple of stretches.

venue_notes: TEG's 4th visit to Catalonia, Spain — well-trodden ground at PGA Catalunya (5th and 6th TEG rounds at the Tour course; 6th and 7th at the Stadium). The course records that fell mean something here precisely because 27 prior Stadium rounds and 22 prior Tour rounds have been played; both Stadium PBs (Mullin R3, Williams R4) and the course-worst Tour 106 are anchored against a real body of history. Weather/colour to be drawn from the dates if it appears in the verified data; otherwise stick to the parkland/championship contrast between the two courses — the Tour 'forgiving sibling', the Stadium 'world-class test', with R1–2 carnage on the supposedly easier of the pair.
course_history_notes: Mullin's new PGA Catalunya - Stadium course PB in R3: 84 gross, improved by 7 — and the new TEG course record across 27 prior visits
Williams's new PGA Catalunya - Stadium PB in R4: 84 gross, improved by 8 — 8 shots better than his last visit
Alex Baker's R2 106 at the Tour: the new course-worst across 22 prior TEG visits
Patterson's twin PBs: 93 gross at the Stadium (R3) and 94 at the Tour (R2)
Jon Baker 8 shots worse than his last visit to the Stadium; Mullin 10 shots worse at the Tour
decisive_moments: Trophy: R1 H5 at the Tour — Alex Baker takes outright Trophy lead and is never headed across the next 67 holes
Green Jacket: R3 H2 at the Stadium — Gregg Williams takes outright Jacket lead with a par and pulls clear (eventually +14)
Wooden Spoon: R3 H2 at the Stadium — Jon Baker triple-bogeys the 2nd to drop to last and never escapes

payoffs.seed: Alex Baker — 3 prior Spoons, Spoons in 2 of the last 3
payoffs.resolves_in: How the Trophy was won + R4
payoffs.payoff: Wins the Trophy on 169 pts by 8 — the serial Spooner crowned, despite a catalogue of double-figure holes that would have buried any net-vs-par run

payoffs.seed: Gregg Williams — reigning Wooden Spoon holder from TEG 17
payoffs.resolves_in: How the Jacket was won + R3
payoffs.payoff: Takes outright Jacket lead at R3 H2 and wins by 14 — Spoon to Jacket inside one TEG

payoffs.seed: Jon Baker — defending Trophy champion from TEG 17
payoffs.resolves_in: How the Spoon was lost + R3 + R4
payoffs.payoff: Drops to Spoon bottom at R3 H2 and stays there; finishes on 126 pts — Trophy holder to Spoon in twelve months

payoffs.seed: Alex's R1 catalogue (7 at the 2nd, 9 at the 18th) piling up while he led
payoffs.resolves_in: R2 + closing 'how the Trophy held' section
payoffs.payoff: R2 doubles down — an 8 at the par-3 16th, an 11 at the 18th, a course-worst 106 — and the Trophy lead drops by exactly one point. Stableford's forgiveness, made literal.
